item.title: For our gatherings with @polene_paris in Tokyo, I wanted to bring the feeling of...
item.description:
🖼️ Slideshow (11 medier)

@olaszwalek

For our gatherings with @polene_paris in Tokyo, I wanted to bring the feeling of an early spring field into the space-through taste, texture, and atmosphere. The dishes grew from that moment of awakening. Root vegetables, first greens, and the subtle bitterness of early spring.

Delphine Parodi @delphineparodi created a wine list that mirrored this sensory journey-unfolding like a landscape, moving from fresh marine notes, through herbal aromatics, and settling into the deep, mineral resonance of Auvergne’s volcanic soils.

I was also so very lucky to include Siberian sturgeon caviar into the course. Provided by Antonius Caviar @antoniuscaviar i- that supported my vision of creating a dialogue between earthy and nutty Japanese yam and complex, delicate yet long taste of their Oscietra.

Chocolate I used for the dessert comes from dear friend @kabukiyusuke - whose work I deeply admire and products I swear by. Thank you once again for your trust and suport.

All the ceramics are made by Kei Condo @keicondo
All the textiles including aprons Ryuichi and Hitomi Okuno @waraya.jp Plants Takuma Saito @hljod_tokyo

Food @olaszwalek support @a_krkw
Wine direction @delphineparodi support @leo.nagashima
Support @tomboy_mai @lisa.gram1995 @makoto_mizu

Photography Nanami Okamoto @___7.umi_

Se slideshow på Instagram
item.content:encoded:
🖼️ Slideshow (11 medier)

@olaszwalek

For our gatherings with @polene_paris in Tokyo, I wanted to bring the feeling of an early spring field into the space-through taste, texture, and atmosphere. The dishes grew from that moment of awakening. Root vegetables, first greens, and the subtle bitterness of early spring.

Delphine Parodi @delphineparodi created a wine list that mirrored this sensory journey-unfolding like a landscape, moving from fresh marine notes, through herbal aromatics, and settling into the deep, mineral resonance of Auvergne’s volcanic soils.

I was also so very lucky to include Siberian sturgeon caviar into the course. Provided by Antonius Caviar @antoniuscaviar i- that supported my vision of creating a dialogue between earthy and nutty Japanese yam and complex, delicate yet long taste of their Oscietra.

Chocolate I used for the dessert comes from dear friend @kabukiyusuke - whose work I deeply admire and products I swear by. Thank you once again for your trust and suport.

All the ceramics are made by Kei Condo @keicondo
All the textiles including aprons Ryuichi and Hitomi Okuno @waraya.jp Plants Takuma Saito @hljod_tokyo

Food @olaszwalek support @a_krkw

Support @tomboy_mai @lisa.gram1995 @makoto_mizu

Photography Nanami Okamoto @___7.umi_

item.author: olaszwalek

For our gatherings with @polene_paris in Tokyo, I wanted to bring the feeling of an early spring field into the space-through taste, texture, and atmosphere. The dishes grew from that moment of awakening. Root vegetables, first greens, and the subtle bitterness of early spring.

Delphine Parodi @delphineparodi created a wine list that mirrored this sensory journey-unfolding like a landscape, moving from fresh marine notes, through herbal aromatics, and settling into the deep, mineral resonance of Auvergne’s volcanic soils.

I was also so very lucky to include Siberian sturgeon caviar into the course. Provided by Antonius Caviar @antoniuscaviar i- that supported my vision of creating a dialogue between earthy and nutty Japanese yam and complex, delicate yet long taste of their Oscietra.

Chocolate I used for the dessert comes from dear friend @kabukiyusuke - whose work I deeply admire and products I swear by. Thank you once again for your trust and suport.

All the ceramics are made by Kei Condo @keicondo

item.content:encoded:
🖼️ Slideshow (19 medier)

@olaszwalek

For our gatherings with @polene_paris in Tokyo, I wanted to bring the feeling of an early spring field into the space-through taste, texture, and atmosphere. The dishes grew from that moment of awakening. Root vegetables, first greens, and the subtle bitterness of early spring.

Delphine Parodi @delphineparodi created a wine list that mirrored this sensory journey-unfolding like a landscape, moving from fresh marine notes, through herbal aromatics, and settling into the deep, mineral resonance of Auvergne’s volcanic soils.

I was also so very lucky to include Siberian sturgeon caviar into the course. Provided by Antonius Caviar @antoniuscaviar i- that supported my vision of creating a dialogue between earthy and nutty Japanese yam and complex, delicate yet long taste of their Oscietra.

Chocolate I used for the dessert comes from dear friend @kabukiyusuke - whose work I deeply admire and products I swear by. Thank you once again for your trust and suport.

All the ceramics are made by Kei Condo @keicondo
All the textiles including aprons Ryuichi and Hitomi Okuno @waraya.jp Plants Takuma Saito @hljod_tokyo

item.author: olaszwalek

item.title: Thank you @polene_paris for giving me the opportunity to dream and bring togethe...
item.description:
🖼️ Slideshow (17 medier)

@olaszwalek

Thank you @polene_paris for giving me the opportunity to dream and bring together a team of people to dream with.

To be able to create this landscape together with a group of incredible artists I get to call friends and on top of that to be able to express myself through creating a menu that has been an extension of it- feels like a greatest gift.

Together with Ryuichi and Takuma - we brought to life this serene scene, that carried a gently wind of early spring. Earth slowly waking up.

This project has been a true celebration of craftsmanship and materials- core elements of Polène. It was a privilege to enter a creative dialogue with each artist and observing a conversation between all crafts- a journey on its own.

Textiles for this project have been specially made @waraya.jp - Ryuchi Okuno and his wife Hitomi. Dyed with Sakura branches that represent spring in Japan.

Takuma Saito from @hljod_tokyo embodied a dream of wild fields by creating plant arrangements.

Kei Condo @keicondo made 60 pieces of special ceramics with a clay from Kasama, Ibaraki- where his studio is based. A unique glaze marked a presence of Sakura- representing it in its gently faded shades.

Washi papers made by talented makers from @sora_to_umi_official based in Chiba

Lights that build a bridge between an interior and nature by @takeshiawaya_ based in Yokohama

@karimoku_official furniture represented a Japanese craftsmanship, quality and timeless design while providing a comfort and pleasure.

Thank you Mai @tomboy_mai for your great mind that finds all possible solutions, being the greatest support one could ask for.

Thank you @a_krkw for being an extension of my hands and mind in the kitchen. I am so lucky to have you.

Thank you @vacant.vc for your trust.

Textiles @ryuichi_okuno @waraya.jp
Plants @hljod_tokyo
Ceramics @keicondo
Lights @takeshiawaya_
Washi @sora_to_umi_official
Furniture @karimoku_official
Production @olaszwalek @tomboy_mai
Chef @olaszwalek
Chefs assistant @a_krkw
Wine @delphineparodi
Menu design @lorenzodalbosco_
Photographer @___7.umi_
Space @vacant.vc
Support @leo.nagashima @lisa.gram1995 @makoto_mizu

🤍🌾

Se slideshow på Instagram
item.content:encoded:
🖼️ Slideshow (17 medier)

@olaszwalek

Thank you @polene_paris for giving me the opportunity to dream and bring together a team of people to dream with.

To be able to create this landscape together with a group of incredible artists I get to call friends and on top of that to be able to express myself through creating a menu that has been an extension of it- feels like a greatest gift.

Together with Ryuichi and Takuma - we brought to life this serene scene, that carried a gently wind of early spring. Earth slowly waking up.

This project has been a true celebration of craftsmanship and materials- core elements of Polène. It was a privilege to enter a creative dialogue with each artist and observing a conversation between all crafts- a journey on its own.

Textiles for this project have been specially made @waraya.jp - Ryuchi Okuno and his wife Hitomi. Dyed with Sakura branches that represent spring in Japan.

Takuma Saito from @hljod_tokyo embodied a dream of wild fields by creating plant arrangements.

Kei Condo @keicondo made 60 pieces of special ceramics with a clay from Kasama, Ibaraki- where his studio is based. A unique glaze marked a presence of Sakura- representing it in its gently faded shades.

Washi papers made by talented makers from @sora_to_umi_official based in Chiba

Lights that build a bridge between an interior and nature by @takeshiawaya_ based in Yokohama

@karimoku_official furniture represented a Japanese craftsmanship, quality and timeless design while providing a comfort and pleasure.

Thank you Mai @tomboy_mai for your great mind that finds all possible solutions, being the greatest support one could ask for.

Thank you @a_krkw for being an extension of my hands and mind in the kitchen. I am so lucky to have you.

Thank you @vacant.vc for your trust.

Textiles @ryuichi_okuno @waraya.jp
Plants @hljod_tokyo
Ceramics @keicondo
Lights @takeshiawaya_
Washi @sora_to_umi_official
Furniture @karimoku_official
Production @olaszwalek @tomboy_mai
Chef @olaszwalek
Chefs assistant @a_krkw
Wine @delphineparodi
Menu design @lorenzodalbosco_
Photographer @___7.umi_
Space @vacant.vc
Support @leo.nagashima @lisa.gram1995 @makoto_mizu

🤍🌾
